bug_report.summary: File tree selected item highlight style/color changes (purple outline -> green outline) outside Assets window scope
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app and navigate to the left sidebar file tree (e.g., Zettlr Tutorial).
Click/select a note such as “Helpful Links”.
Observe the selection/focus highlight styling around the selected item.
expected_behaviors: Selection highlight styling in the main file tree remains unchanged by an Assets window-only styling cleanup.
Selected item highlight should match the established theme/colors used elsewhere in the main window.
actual_behaviors: The selected item highlight appears to change from a purple outline/background style (before) to a green outline style (after) around “Helpful Links” (diff area bbox [61,230,153,259]).

bug_report.summary: Unexpected small red 'Y'/marker appears in editor gutter near YAML frontmatter area
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app and open a note containing YAML frontmatter.
Look at the left gutter/margin area next to the YAML frontmatter lines (near the top of the document).
expected_behaviors: No additional stray character/marker should appear in the editor gutter unless intentionally part of linting/diagnostics UI (and consistent with prior behavior).
actual_behaviors: A small red 'Y'/marker appears in the editor gutter area (diff bbox [367,247,383,261]) that is not present before the change.

bug_report.summary: Right editor pane vertical scrollbar/thumb renders differently after change (possible unintended global styling leak)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app to the main editor window.
Open the File menu (so the main editor layout remains visible as in the screenshot).
Observe the far-right edge of the upper-right editor pane (Split View preview) vertical scrollbar area.
expected_behaviors: Scrollbar/thumb rendering and position should be consistent and unchanged by Assets window styling cleanup.
No visual artifact/shift in the scrollbar thumb area between builds.
actual_behaviors: A visible change occurs in the vertical scrollbar/thumb region on the far right of the upper-right pane (difference at approx. x=895–905, y=538–584), indicating the scrollbar/thumb appears shifted/altered between before vs after.

bug_report.summary: Lua Filters list truncates newly created filter name with ellipsis (e.g., “filter-smoke...”)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets Manager.
Go to the Lua Filter tab.
Create a new filter.
Enter the name “filter-smoke-001” and confirm (press Enter).
Observe the new filter entry in the left list.
expected_behaviors: The filter name should be fully visible in the left list when it fits (e.g., “filter-smoke-001”), consistent with pre-change rendering/available space.
actual_behaviors: The new filter entry appears truncated with an ellipsis (“filter-smoke...”) in the left list after the change, reducing readability.

bug_report.summary: “Rename filter” button appears disabled even when a Lua filter is selected
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets Manager.
Go to the Lua Filter tab.
Select a filter in the left list (e.g., the newly created “filter-smoke-001”).
Observe the Rename control/button next to the filename field.
expected_behaviors: When a filter is selected, the “Rename filter” control should be enabled (unless there is a clear reason such as a protected/read-only filter, accompanied by messaging).
actual_behaviors: A filter is visibly selected and its filename is shown in the name field, but the “Rename filter” button appears disabled/greyed out.

false_positive_reasons: Unreasonable or unnecessary improvement suggestion, not a real issue

bug_report.summary: Assets Manager opens on a different/incorrect tab after tab order change (tab persistence likely broken)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Preferences → Assets Manager.
Close it (optional) and open it again (or restart app if tab selection is persisted across sessions).
expected_behaviors: Assets Manager should open consistently on the intended default tab, or restore the same previously-selected tab (by tab identity), regardless of tab order changes.
actual_behaviors: Assets Manager opens to a different tab between builds; in the after-change screenshot it opens on “Custom CSS” whereas before it opened on “Lua Filter,” suggesting restored tab selection is incorrect after reordering.

bug_report.summary: After saving Custom CSS and waiting, the footer status area does not clear/collapse (empty bordered box remains)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Preferences → Assets Manager.
Go to the “Custom CSS” tab.
Make any edit in the CSS editor.
Click “Save”.
Wait ~1 second without interacting.
expected_behaviors: A save confirmation should appear (per unified messaging) and then clear after the configured duration.
After the message clears, the status area should collapse/stop rendering so there is no empty bordered element next to the Save button.
actual_behaviors: After ~1 second, a visible empty bordered rectangle remains next to the Save button, indicating the status container is still rendered even when empty.

false_positive_reasons: Unreasonable or unnecessary improvement suggestion, not a real issue

bug_report.summary: Filename field selection/focus state differs after triple-click (after-change selects filename, before-change does not)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets Manager
Select an existing defaults item (e.g., test_item)
Triple-click inside the filename text field (showing e.g., test_item.yaml)
expected_behaviors: Triple-clicking the filename field should result in consistent selection/focus behavior across builds (either selects the text or does not, but consistently).
actual_behaviors: Before change: filename text is not visibly selected/focused after triple-click.
After change: filename text is visibly selected (highlighted) after triple-click.

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch

bug_report.summary: Invalid defaults name appears to be created/listed after rename attempt with invalid name (e.g., “invalid/name”)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets Manager
Create/select a defaults file (e.g., test_item)
In the filename field, enter an invalid name containing a slash: "invalid/name"
Click "Rename file"
expected_behaviors: Rename should fail validation and show error feedback.
No new defaults item should appear in the left list for an invalid name.
actual_behaviors: After change, a new item appears in the left list with a name starting with "invalid-n…" (suggesting the invalid name was accepted/created), and no failure feedback is visible in the screenshot.
